violations.finding: Hardcoded absolute repo path: cwd='/dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude'. Same pattern repeated at line 37 and in test_AC2:21, test_AC3:23, test_AC4:24, test_AC5:21. Five new test files all embed the dev machine ramdisk path directly.
violations.recommendation: Derive repo root dynamically: REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3], then use cwd=REPO and build file paths from REPO. This ensures tests are portable across machines.
violations.note: Scope advisory: test files live in tests/generated/ rather than scripts/; Standard 2 formally targets scripts/*.py. However these are newly introduced in this cycle and the hardcoded /dev/shm path is a genuine portability defect. Codex flagged this with PROPOSED_FIX. Logged as critical advisory finding.

observations.finding: LOG=/var/log/tmp-cleanup.log is a hardcoded system path with no parameter override. Retention thresholds (-mtime +1, +3, +7) and system paths (/tmp, /var/tmp, /var/tmp/codex-outputs) throughout the file are also unparameterized.
observations.disposition: WAIVED-BY-AC3: This file is the version-controlled mirror of /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh; AC3 (test_AC3) requires line-2..EOF content to be IDENTICAL to the source file. Parameterizing any of these values would break the AC3 parity requirement. Do not count as a blocking Standard 2 violation unless the mirror requirement is relaxed in a future cycle.
observations.note: Codex confirmed this is a false positive / waived finding given the AC3 mirror constraint.

standard_3_use_source_venv.note: scripts/score-update.sh:74 activates venv on same line as python3 invocation. Line 73 comment explicitly documents this pattern. CLEAN.

standard_8_concise_documentation.note: No .claude/commands or .claude/agents files changed in this cycle.

standard_9_parameterized_scripts.note: score-update.sh accepts --agent, --event, --note, --scores-file parameters. tmp-cleanup-install.sh is AC3-waived mirror. Test files have advisory hardcoded paths.

standard_10_dangling_references.note: scripts/score-update.sh and scripts/install/tmp-cleanup-install.sh both exist. No new dangling references.

summary.note: 1 critical advisory finding (hardcoded /dev/shm repo path in 5 new test files) introduced_in_diff: true. 1 Standard 2 waived by AC3 mirror constraint. All pre-existing violations have introduced_in_diff: false and do not block close.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 3 PROPOSED_FIX findings. (1) tmp-cleanup-install.sh LOG path is false positive waived by AC3 mirror requirement -- applied. (2) Under-reporting resolved by applying AC3 waiver. (3) Hardcoded /dev/shm paths in 5 new test files is Standard 2 portability issue -- applied as new critical advisory finding with introduced_in_diff: true.

findings.issue: scripts/install/tmp-cleanup-install.sh:40 LOG path is a false positive -- AC3 requires line-2..EOF mirror parity; parameterizing would break AC3.
findings.proposed_fix: OBSERVATION_ONLY / WAIVED-BY-AC3: do not count LOG=/var/log/tmp-cleanup.log as blocking Standard 2 violation unless mirror requirement relaxed.
findings.rationale: AC3 acceptance criterion binds the file to be content-identical to the system script. Counting fixed system paths as Standard 2 violations forces a conflict with AC3; AC3 is the binding delivery requirement for this cycle.

findings.issue: If waiver not applied, draft under-reports: many more hardcoded paths/constants in the mirror would also be violations.
findings.proposed_fix: Either apply AC3 waiver or report Standard 2 + Standard 9 for entire file.
findings.rationale: Applied the AC3 waiver path. Full-file Standard 2/9 escalation is the fallback if waiver rejected by close.md.

findings.issue: Hardcoded /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude in 5 new test files (test_AC1:25,37; test_AC2:21; test_AC3:23; test_AC4:24; test_AC5:21).
findings.proposed_fix: Derive repo root dynamically via REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]; pass cwd=REPO.
findings.rationale: Concrete PROPOSED_FIX from Codex. Finding is real -- tests fail on any machine where repo is not at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude. Added as critical advisory finding with introduced_in_diff: true.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 (tmp-cleanup LOG path): applied waiver, downgraded from critical violation to observation. Finding 2 (under-reporting): resolved by accepting waiver. Finding 3 (hardcoded test paths): added as new critical advisory finding with introduced_in_diff: true.
